No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides quality tertiary education and equips students with relevant financial skills for employment and successful business careers (Targets 4.3, 4.4).
evidence: foundation for all advanced business school classes

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides foundational skills for economic productivity, entrepreneurship, and understanding financial services, crucial for 'building a successful business career' (Targets 8.2, 8.3, 8.10).
evidence: accounting for business, government, non-profit organizations

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course covers accounting for 'Property, Plant, and Equipment' (Target 9.1) and provides financial literacy essential for enterprises to access financial services (Target 9.3).
evidence: Investments: Property, Plant, and Equipment and Intangible Assets
accounting for assets, liabilities, and shareholders’ equity

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Understanding financial accounting rules and analysis can indirectly contribute to informing fiscal policies and financial market regulation, relevant to reducing inequalities (Targets 10.4, 10.5).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course mentions accounting for 'government, non-profit organizations,' which has a very indirect and minor connection to managing cities and communities.
evidence: accounting for business, government, non-profit organizations

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course provides foundational knowledge in financial reporting and internal controls, essential for companies to integrate sustainability information into their reporting cycles (Target 12.6).
evidence: Internal Controls: Ensuring the Integrity of Financial Information

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on 'Internal Controls: Ensuring the Integrity of Financial Information' directly supports developing effective, accountable, and transparent institutions and reducing corruption (Targets 16.5, 16.6).
evidence: Internal Controls: Ensuring the Integrity of Financial Information
develop effective, accountable and transparent institutions

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Accounting skills are fundamental for domestic resource mobilization (Target 17.1) and providing data for macroeconomic stability and progress measurement (Targets 17.13, 17.19), as financial reports are used by government.
evidence: The use and interpretation of financial reports
